A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_interrupt_event.processed_at)
session_thread_id: optional stringî‚š
If absent, interrupts every non-archived thread in a multiagent session (or the primary alone in a single-agent session). If present, interrupts only the named thread.

[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_interrupt_event)

BetaManagedAgentsUserToolConfirmationEvent object { id, result, tool_use_id, 4 more } î‚š
A tool confirmation event that approves or denies a pending tool execution.
Unique identifier for this event.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event.id)
result: "allow" or "deny"î‚š
UserToolConfirmationResult enum
"allow"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event.result%5B0%5D)
"deny"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event.result%5B1%5D)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event.result)
tool_use_id: stringî‚š
The id of the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event this result corresponds to, which can be found in the last `session.status_idle` [event's](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/sessions/events/list#beta_managed_agents_session_requires_action.event_ids) `stop_reason.event_ids` field.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event.tool_use_id)
type: "user.tool_confirmation"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event.type)
deny_message: optional stringî‚š
Optional message providing context for a 'deny' decision. Only allowed when result is 'deny'.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event.deny_message)
processed_at: optional stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event.processed_at)
session_thread_id: optional stringî‚š
When set, the confirmation routes to this subagent's thread rather than the primary. Echo this from the `session_thread_id` on the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event that prompted the approval.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event.session_thread_id)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event)

BetaManagedAgentsUserToolResultEventParams object { tool_use_id, type, content, is_error } î‚š
Parameters for providing the result of an agent-toolset tool execution. Only valid on `self_hosted` environments, where sandbox-routed tools are executed by the client rather than the server.
tool_use_id: stringî‚š
The id of the `agent.tool_use` event this result corresponds to, which can be found in the last `session.status_idle` [event's](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/sessions/events/list#beta_managed_agents_session_requires_action.event_ids) `stop_reason.event_ids` field.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_result_event_params.tool_use_id)
type: "user.tool_result"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_result_event_params.type)

####  BetaSessionsResources
##### [Add Session Resource](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/sessions/resources/add)

##### [List Session Resources](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/sessions/resources/list)

##### [Update Session Resource](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/sessions/resources/update)

##### [Delete Session Resource](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/sessions/resources/delete)

BetaManagedAgentsMemoryStoreResource object { memory_store_id, type, access, 4 more } î‚š
A memory store attached to an agent session.
memory_store_id: stringî‚š
The memory store ID (memstore_...). Must belong to the caller's organization and workspace.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource.memory_store_id)
type: "memory_store"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource.type)
access: optional "read_write" or "read_only"î‚š
Access mode for an attached memory store.
"read_write"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource.access%5B0%5D)
"read_only"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource.access%5B1%5D)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource.access)
description: optional stringî‚š
Description of the memory store, snapshotted at attach time. Rendered into the agent's system prompt. Empty string when the store has no description.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource.description)
instructions: optional stringî‚š
Per-attachment guidance for the agent on how to use this store. Rendered into the memory section of the system prompt. Max 4096 chars.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource.instructions)
mount_path: optional stringî‚š
Filesystem path where the store is mounted in the session container, e.g. /mnt/memory/user-preferences. Derived from the store's name. Output-only.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource.mount_path)
name: optional stringî‚š
Display name of the memory store, snapshotted at attach time. Later edits to the store's name do not propagate to this resource.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource.name)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource)

BetaManagedAgentsMemoryStoreResource object { memory_store_id, type, access, 4 more } î‚š
A memory store attached to an agent session.
memory_store_id: stringî‚š
The memory store ID (memstore_...). Must belong to the caller's organization and workspace.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource.memory_store_id)
type: "memory_store"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource.type)
access: optional "read_write" or "read_only"î‚š
Access mode for an attached memory store.
"read_write"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource.access%5B0%5D)
"read_only"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource.access%5B1%5D)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource.access)
description: optional stringî‚š
Description of the memory store, snapshotted at attach time. Rendered into the agent's system prompt. Empty string when the store has no description.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource.description)
instructions: optional stringî‚š
Per-attachment guidance for the agent on how to use this store. Rendered into the memory section of the system prompt. Max 4096 chars.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource.instructions)
mount_path: optional stringî‚š
Filesystem path where the store is mounted in the session container, e.g. /mnt/memory/user-preferences. Derived from the store's name. Output-only.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource.mount_path)
name: optional stringî‚š
Display name of the memory store, snapshotted at attach time. Later edits to the store's name do not propagate to this resource.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource.name)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#resource_update_response)
####  BetaSessionsThreads
##### [List Session Threads](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/sessions/threads/list)
GET/v1/sessions/{session_id}/threads
##### [Get Session Thread](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/sessions/threads/retrieve)
GET/v1/sessions/{session_id}/threads/{thread_id}
##### [Archive Session Thread](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/sessions/threads/archive)
POST/v1/sessions/{session_id}/threads/{thread_id}/archive
##### ModelsExpand Collapse 
BetaManagedAgentsSessionThread object { id, agent, archived_at, 8 more } î‚š
An execution thread within a `session`. Each session has one primary thread plus zero or more child threads spawned by the coordinator.
Unique identifier for this thread.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_thread.id)
agent: [BetaManagedAgentsSessionThreadAgent](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_thread_agent) { id, description, mcp_servers, 7 more } î‚š
Resolved `agent` definition for a single `session_thread`. Snapshot of the agent at thread creation time. The multiagent roster is not repeated here; read it from `Session.agent`.

BetaManagedAgentsUserInterruptEvent object { id, type, processed_at, session_thread_id } î‚š
An interrupt event that pauses agent execution and returns control to the user.
Unique identifier for this event.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_interrupt_event.id)
type: "user.interrupt"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_interrupt_event.type)
processed_at: optional stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_interrupt_event.processed_at)
session_thread_id: optional stringî‚š
If absent, interrupts every non-archived thread in a multiagent session (or the primary alone in a single-agent session). If present, interrupts only the named thread.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_interrupt_event.session_thread_id)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_interrupt_event)
BetaManagedAgentsUserToolConfirmationEvent object { id, result, tool_use_id, 4 more } î‚š
A tool confirmation event that approves or denies a pending tool execution.
Unique identifier for this event.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event.id)
result: "allow" or "deny"î‚š
UserToolConfirmationResult enum
"allow"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event.result%5B0%5D)
"deny"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event.result%5B1%5D)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event.result)
tool_use_id: stringî‚š
The id of the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event this result corresponds to, which can be found in the last `session.status_idle` [event's](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/sessions/events/list#beta_managed_agents_session_requires_action.event_ids) `stop_reason.event_ids` field.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event.tool_use_id)
type: "user.tool_confirmation"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event.type)
deny_message: optional stringî‚š
Optional message providing context for a 'deny' decision. Only allowed when result is 'deny'.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event.deny_message)
processed_at: optional stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event.processed_at)
session_thread_id: optional stringî‚š
When set, the confirmation routes to this subagent's thread rather than the primary. Echo this from the `session_thread_id` on the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event that prompted the approval.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event.session_thread_id)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_tool_confirmation_event)
BetaManagedAgentsUserCustomToolResultEvent object { id, custom_tool_use_id, type, 4 more } î‚š
Event sent by the client providing the result of a custom tool execution.
Unique identifier for this event.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_custom_tool_result_event.id)
custom_tool_use_id: stringî‚š

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

Routes this result to a subagent thread. Copy from the `agent.custom_tool_use` event's `session_thread_id`.

[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_custom_tool_result_event)
BetaManagedAgentsAgentCustomToolUseEvent object { id, input, name, 3 more } î‚š
Event emitted when the agent calls a custom tool. The session goes idle until the client sends a `user.custom_tool_result` event with the result.
Unique identifier for this event.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_custom_tool_use_event.id)
input: map[unknown]î‚š
Input parameters for the tool call.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_custom_tool_use_event.input)
name: stringî‚š
Name of the custom tool being called.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_custom_tool_use_event.name)
processed_at: stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_custom_tool_use_event.processed_at)
type: "agent.custom_tool_use"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_custom_tool_use_event.type)
session_thread_id: optional stringî‚š
When set, this event was cross-posted from a subagent's thread to surface its custom tool use on the primary thread's stream. Empty on the thread's own events. Echo this on a `user.custom_tool_result` event to route the result back.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_custom_tool_use_event.session_thread_id)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_custom_tool_use_event)

Name of the MCP tool being used.

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

type: "agent.mcp_tool_use"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_mcp_tool_use_event.type)

AgentEvaluatedPermission enum
"allow"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_mcp_tool_use_event.evaluated_permission%5B0%5D)
"ask"î‚š

[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_mcp_tool_use_event.evaluated_permission)
session_thread_id: optional stringî‚š
When set, this event was cross-posted from a subagent's thread to surface its permission request on the primary thread's stream. Empty on the thread's own events. Echo this on a `user.tool_confirmation` event to route the approval back.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_mcp_tool_use_event.session_thread_id)

BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolUseEvent object { id, input, name, 4 more } î‚š
Event emitted when the agent invokes a built-in agent tool.
Unique identifier for this event.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_tool_use_event.id)
input: map[unknown]î‚š
Input parameters for the tool call.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_tool_use_event.input)
name: stringî‚š
Name of the agent tool being used.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_tool_use_event.name)
processed_at: stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_tool_use_event.processed_at)
type: "agent.tool_use"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_tool_use_event.type)
evaluated_permission: optional "allow" or "ask" or "deny"î‚š
AgentEvaluatedPermission enum
"allow"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_tool_use_event.evaluated_permission%5B0%5D)
"ask"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_tool_use_event.evaluated_permission%5B1%5D)
"deny"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_tool_use_event.evaluated_permission%5B2%5D)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_tool_use_event.evaluated_permission)
session_thread_id: optional stringî‚š
When set, this event was cross-posted from a subagent's thread to surface its permission request on the primary thread's stream. Empty on the thread's own events. Echo this on a `user.tool_confirmation` event to route the approval back.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_tool_use_event.session_thread_id)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_agent_tool_use_event)

The server is retrying automatically. Client should wait; the same error type may fire again as retrying, then once as exhausted when the retry budget runs out.

BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusExhausted object { type } î‚š
This turn is dead; queued inputs are flushed and the session returns to idle. Client may send a new prompt.

BetaManagedAgentsSessionRequiresAction object { event_ids, type } î‚š

BetaManagedAgentsSpanOutcomeEvaluationStartEvent object { id, iteration, outcome_id, 2 more } î‚š
Emitted when an outcome evaluation cycle begins.
Unique identifier for this event.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_start_event.id)
iteration: numberî‚š
0-indexed revision cycle. 0 is the first evaluation; 1 is the re-evaluation after the first revision; etc.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_start_event.iteration)
outcome_id: stringî‚š
The `outc_` ID of the outcome being evaluated.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_start_event.outcome_id)
processed_at: stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_start_event.processed_at)
type: "span.outcome_evaluation_start"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_start_event.type)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_start_event)
BetaManagedAgentsSpanOutcomeEvaluationEndEvent object { id, explanation, iteration, 6 more } î‚š
Emitted when an outcome evaluation cycle completes. Carries the verdict and aggregate token usage. A verdict of `needs_revision` means another evaluation cycle follows; `satisfied`, `max_iterations_reached`, `failed`, or `interrupted` are terminal â€” no further evaluation cycles follow.
Unique identifier for this event.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_end_event.id)
explanation: stringî‚š
Human-readable explanation of the verdict. For `needs_revision`, describes which criteria failed and why.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_end_event.explanation)
iteration: numberî‚š
0-indexed revision cycle, matching the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start`.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_end_event.iteration)
outcome_evaluation_start_id: stringî‚š
The id of the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start` event.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_end_event.outcome_evaluation_start_id)
outcome_id: stringî‚š
The `outc_` ID of the outcome being evaluated.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_end_event.outcome_id)
processed_at: stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_end_event.processed_at)
result: stringî‚š
Evaluation verdict. 'satisfied': criteria met, session goes idle. 'needs_revision': criteria not met, another revision cycle follows. 'max_iterations_reached': evaluation budget exhausted with criteria still unmet â€” one final acknowledgment turn follows before the session goes idle, but no further evaluation runs. 'failed': grader determined the rubric does not apply to the deliverables. 'interrupted': user sent an interrupt while evaluation was in progress.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_end_event.result)
type: "span.outcome_evaluation_end"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_end_event.type)
usage: [BetaManagedAgentsSpanModelUsage](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_model_usage) { cache_creation_input_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, input_tokens, 2 more } î‚š
Token usage for a single model request.
cache_creation_input_tokens: numberî‚š
Tokens used to create prompt cache in this request.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_end_event.usage%20%2B%20\(resource\)%20beta.sessions.events.cache_creation_input_tokens)
cache_read_input_tokens: numberî‚š
Tokens read from prompt cache in this request.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_end_event.usage%20%2B%20\(resource\)%20beta.sessions.events.cache_read_input_tokens)
input_tokens: numberî‚š
Input tokens consumed by this request.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_end_event.usage%20%2B%20\(resource\)%20beta.sessions.events.input_tokens)
output_tokens: numberî‚š
Output tokens generated by this request.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_end_event.usage%20%2B%20\(resource\)%20beta.sessions.events.output_tokens)
speed: optional "standard" or "fast"î‚š
Inference speed mode. `fast` provides significantly faster output token generation at premium pricing. Not all models support `fast`; invalid combinations are rejected at create time.
"standard"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_end_event.usage%20%2B%20\(resource\)%20beta.sessions.events.speed%5B0%5D)
"fast"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_end_event.usage%20%2B%20\(resource\)%20beta.sessions.events.speed%5B1%5D)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_end_event.usage%20%2B%20\(resource\)%20beta.sessions.events.speed)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_end_event.usage)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_end_event)

BetaManagedAgentsSpanOutcomeEvaluationOngoingEvent object { id, iteration, outcome_id, 2 more } î‚š
Periodic heartbeat emitted while an outcome evaluation cycle is in progress. Distinguishes 'evaluation is actively running' from 'evaluation is stuck' between the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start` and `span.outcome_evaluation_end` events.
Unique identifier for this event.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_ongoing_event.id)
iteration: numberî‚š
0-indexed revision cycle, matching the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start`.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_span_outcome_evaluation_ongoing_event.iteration)
outcome_id: stringî‚š
The `outc_` ID of the outcome being evaluated.

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

BetaManagedAgentsUserDefineOutcomeEvent object { id, description, max_iterations, 4 more } î‚š
Echo of a `user.define_outcome` input event. Carries the server-generated `outcome_id` that subsequent `span.outcome_evaluation_*` events reference.
Unique identifier for this event.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_define_outcome_event.id)
description: stringî‚š
What the agent should produce. Copied from the input event.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_define_outcome_event.description)
max_iterations: numberî‚š
Evaluate-then-revise cycles before giving up. Default 3, max 20.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_define_outcome_event.max_iterations)
outcome_id: stringî‚š
Server-generated `outc_` ID for this outcome. Referenced by `span.outcome_evaluation_*` events and the session's `outcome_evaluations` list.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_define_outcome_event.outcome_id)
processed_at: stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_define_outcome_event.processed_at)

Rubric for grading the quality of an outcome.

BetaManagedAgentsSessionThreadStatusRunningEvent object { id, agent_name, processed_at, 2 more } î‚š
A session thread has begun executing. Emitted on the thread's own stream and cross-posted to the primary stream for child threads.

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

session_thread_id: stringî‚š
Public sthr_ ID of the thread that started running.

BetaManagedAgentsSessionThreadStatusIdleEvent object { id, agent_name, processed_at, 3 more } î‚š
A session thread has yielded and is awaiting input. Emitted on the thread's own stream and cross-posted to the primary stream for child threads.
Unique identifier for this event.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_thread_status_idle_event.id)

Name of the agent the thread runs.

processed_at: stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_thread_status_idle_event.processed_at)
session_thread_id: stringî‚š
Public sthr_ ID of the thread that went idle.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_thread_status_idle_event.session_thread_id)
stop_reason: [BetaManagedAgentsSessionEndTurn](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_end_turn) { type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsSessionRequiresAction](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_requires_action) { event_ids, type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsSessionRetriesExhausted](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_retries_exhausted) { type } î‚š
The agent completed its turn naturally and is ready for the next user message.
BetaManagedAgentsSessionEndTurn object { type } î‚š
The agent completed its turn naturally and is ready for the next user message.
type: "end_turn"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_end_turn.type)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_end_turn)
BetaManagedAgentsSessionRequiresAction object { event_ids, type } î‚š
The agent is idle waiting on one or more blocking user-input events (tool confirmation, custom tool result, etc.). Resolving all of them transitions the session back to running.
event_ids: array of stringî‚š
The ids of events the agent is blocked on. Resolving fewer than all re-emits `session.status_idle` with the remainder.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_requires_action.event_ids)
type: "requires_action"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_requires_action.type)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_requires_action)
BetaManagedAgentsSessionRetriesExhausted object { type } î‚š
The turn ended because the retry budget was exhausted (`max_iterations` hit or an error escalated to `retry_status: 'exhausted'`).

[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_retries_exhausted.type)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_retries_exhausted)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_thread_status_idle_event.stop_reason)
type: "session.thread_status_idle"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_thread_status_idle_event.type)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_thread_status_idle_event)

BetaManagedAgentsUserToolResultEvent object { id, tool_use_id, type, 4 more } î‚š
Event sent by the client providing the result of an agent-toolset tool execution. Only valid on `self_hosted` environments, where sandbox-routed tools are executed by the client rather than the server.

####  BetaSessionsThreadsEvents
##### [List Session Thread Events](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/sessions/threads/events/list)
GET/v1/sessions/{session_id}/threads/{thread_id}/events
##### [Stream Session Thread Events](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/sessions/threads/events/stream)
GET/v1/sessions/{session_id}/threads/{thread_id}/stream
####  BetaDeployments
##### [Create Deployment](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/deployments/create)
POST/v1/deployments
##### [List Deployments](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/deployments/list)
GET/v1/deployments
##### [Get Deployment](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/deployments/retrieve)
GET/v1/deployments/{deployment_id}
##### [Update Deployment](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/deployments/update)
POST/v1/deployments/{deployment_id}
##### [Archive Deployment](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/deployments/archive)
POST/v1/deployments/{deployment_id}/archive
##### [Run Deployment Now](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/deployments/run)
POST/v1/deployments/{deployment_id}/run
##### [Pause Deployment](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/deployments/pause)
POST/v1/deployments/{deployment_id}/pause
##### [Unpause Deployment](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/deployments/unpause)
POST/v1/deployments/{deployment_id}/unpause

type: "agent_archived_error"î‚š

BetaManagedAgentsCronSchedule object { expression, timezone, type, 2 more } î‚š
5-field POSIX cron schedule with computed runtime timestamps.
expression: stringî‚š
5-field POSIX cron expression: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week (e.g., "0 9 * * 1-5" for weekdays at 9am). Day-of-week is 0-7 where 0 and 7 both mean Sunday. Extended cron syntax - seconds or year fields, and the special characters L, W, #, and ? - is not supported, nor are predefined shortcuts (@daily).
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_cron_schedule.expression)
timezone: stringî‚š
IANA timezone identifier (e.g., "America/Los_Angeles", "UTC").
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_cron_schedule.timezone)
type: "cron"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_cron_schedule.type)
last_run_at: optional stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_cron_schedule.last_run_at)
upcoming_runs_at: optional array of stringî‚š
Up to 5 timestamps of upcoming cron occurrences. Non-empty for active and paused deployments (reflects what the schedule would do if unpaused); empty once the deployment is archived (`archived_at` set). Each fire is offset by a small per-schedule jitter, so a run will actually start at or shortly after its listed time.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_cron_schedule.upcoming_runs_at)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_cron_schedule)
BetaManagedAgentsCronScheduleParams object { expression, timezone, type } î‚š
5-field POSIX cron schedule. Literal wall-clock matching in the configured timezone.
expression: stringî‚š
5-field POSIX cron expression: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week (e.g., "0 9 * * 1-5" for weekdays at 9am). Day-of-week is 0-7 where 0 and 7 both mean Sunday. Extended cron syntax - seconds or year fields, and the special characters L, W, #, and ? - is not supported, nor are predefined shortcuts (@daily).
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_cron_schedule_params.expression)
timezone: stringî‚š
Required. IANA timezone identifier (e.g., "America/Los_Angeles", "UTC"). Validated against the IANA timezone database.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_cron_schedule_params.timezone)
type: "cron"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_cron_schedule_params.type)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_cron_schedule_params)

BetaManagedAgentsMemoryStoreResourceConfig object { memory_store_id, type, access, instructions } î‚š
A memory store attached to each session created from this deployment.

The memory store ID (memstore_...). Must belong to the caller's organization and workspace.

type: "memory_store"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource_config.type)

Access mode for an attached memory store.
"read_write"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource_config.access%5B0%5D)
"read_only"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource_config.access%5B1%5D)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource_config.access)
instructions: optional stringî‚š
Per-attachment guidance for the agent on how to use this store. Rendered into the memory section of the system prompt. Max 4096 chars.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource_config.instructions)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store_resource_config)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_deployment.resources)
schedule: [BetaManagedAgentsSchedule](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_schedule) { expression, timezone, type, 2 more } î‚š
5-field POSIX cron schedule with computed runtime timestamps.
expression: stringî‚š
5-field POSIX cron expression: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week (e.g., "0 9 * * 1-5" for weekdays at 9am). Day-of-week is 0-7 where 0 and 7 both mean Sunday. Extended cron syntax - seconds or year fields, and the special characters L, W, #, and ? - is not supported, nor are predefined shortcuts (@daily).
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_deployment.schedule%20%2B%20\(resource\)%20beta.deployments.expression)
timezone: stringî‚š
IANA timezone identifier (e.g., "America/Los_Angeles", "UTC").
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_deployment.schedule%20%2B%20\(resource\)%20beta.deployments.timezone)
type: "cron"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_deployment.schedule%20%2B%20\(resource\)%20beta.deployments.type)
last_run_at: optional stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_deployment.schedule%20%2B%20\(resource\)%20beta.deployments.last_run_at)
upcoming_runs_at: optional array of stringî‚š
Up to 5 timestamps of upcoming cron occurrences. Non-empty for active and paused deployments (reflects what the schedule would do if unpaused); empty once the deployment is archived (`archived_at` set). Each fire is offset by a small per-schedule jitter, so a run will actually start at or shortly after its listed time.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_deployment.schedule%20%2B%20\(resource\)%20beta.deployments.upcoming_runs_at)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_deployment.schedule)

type: "deployment"î‚š

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_deployment.updated_at)

BetaManagedAgentsSystemMessageEventParams object { content, type } î‚š
Privileged context for the accompanying turn and all subsequent turns, appended to the session's system context as a `role: "system"` turn rather than replacing the top-level system prompt. At most one per request: it must be the final event and immediately follow the `user.message`, `user.tool_result`, or `user.custom_tool_result` it accompanies. Only supported on models that accept mid-conversation system messages.
content: array of [BetaManagedAgentsSystemContentBlock](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_system_content_block) { text, type } î‚š
System content blocks to append. Text-only.
text: stringî‚š
The text content.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_system_content_block.text)
type: "text"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_system_content_block.type)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_system_message_event_params.content)

BetaManagedAgentsSchedule object { expression, timezone, type, 2 more } î‚š
5-field POSIX cron schedule with computed runtime timestamps.
expression: stringî‚š
5-field POSIX cron expression: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week (e.g., "0 9 * * 1-5" for weekdays at 9am). Day-of-week is 0-7 where 0 and 7 both mean Sunday. Extended cron syntax - seconds or year fields, and the special characters L, W, #, and ? - is not supported, nor are predefined shortcuts (@daily).
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_schedule.expression)
timezone: stringî‚š
IANA timezone identifier (e.g., "America/Los_Angeles", "UTC").
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_schedule.timezone)
type: "cron"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_schedule.type)
last_run_at: optional stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_schedule.last_run_at)
upcoming_runs_at: optional array of stringî‚š
Up to 5 timestamps of upcoming cron occurrences. Non-empty for active and paused deployments (reflects what the schedule would do if unpaused); empty once the deployment is archived (`archived_at` set). Each fire is offset by a small per-schedule jitter, so a run will actually start at or shortly after its listed time.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_schedule.upcoming_runs_at)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_schedule)
BetaManagedAgentsScheduleParams object { expression, timezone, type } î‚š
5-field POSIX cron schedule. Literal wall-clock matching in the configured timezone.
expression: stringî‚š
5-field POSIX cron expression: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week (e.g., "0 9 * * 1-5" for weekdays at 9am). Day-of-week is 0-7 where 0 and 7 both mean Sunday. Extended cron syntax - seconds or year fields, and the special characters L, W, #, and ? - is not supported, nor are predefined shortcuts (@daily).
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_schedule_params.expression)
timezone: stringî‚š
Required. IANA timezone identifier (e.g., "America/Los_Angeles", "UTC"). Validated against the IANA timezone database.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_schedule_params.timezone)
type: "cron"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_schedule_params.type)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_schedule_params)

BetaManagedAgentsLimitedCredentialNetworkingParams object { allowed_hosts, type } î‚š
Substitute the secret only on requests to the listed hosts.
allowed_hosts: array of stringî‚š
Hostnames on which the secret will be substituted. Each entry is a bare hostname (`api.example.com`), an IPv4 address (`192.0.2.1`), or a `*.`-prefixed wildcard (`*.example.com`). URLs, ports, paths, and IPv6 addresses are not accepted. At most 16 entries.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_limited_credential_networking_params.allowed_hosts)
type: "limited"î‚š

####  BetaMemory Stores
##### [Create a memory store](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/memory_stores/create)
POST/v1/memory_stores
##### [List memory stores](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/memory_stores/list)
GET/v1/memory_stores
##### [Retrieve a memory store](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/memory_stores/retrieve)
GET/v1/memory_stores/{memory_store_id}
##### [Update a memory store](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/memory_stores/update)
POST/v1/memory_stores/{memory_store_id}
##### [Delete a memory store](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/memory_stores/delete)
DELETE/v1/memory_stores/{memory_store_id}
##### [Archive a memory store](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/memory_stores/archive)
POST/v1/memory_stores/{memory_store_id}/archive
##### ModelsExpand Collapse 
BetaManagedAgentsDeletedMemoryStore object { id, type } î‚š
Confirmation that a `memory_store` was deleted.
ID of the deleted memory store (a `memstore_...` identifier). The store and all its memories and versions are no longer retrievable.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_deleted_memory_store.id)
type: "memory_store_deleted"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_deleted_memory_store.type)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_deleted_memory_store)
BetaManagedAgentsMemoryStore object { id, created_at, name, 5 more } î‚š
A `memory_store`: a named container for agent memories, scoped to a workspace. Attach a store to a session via `resources[]` to mount it as a directory the agent can read and write.
Unique identifier for the memory store (a `memstore_...` tagged ID). Use this when attaching the store to a session, or in the `{memory_store_id}` path parameter of subsequent calls.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store.id)
created_at: stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store.created_at)
name: stringî‚š
Human-readable name for the store. 1â€“255 characters. The store's mount-path slug under `/mnt/memory/` is derived from this name.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store.name)
type: "memory_store"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store.type)
updated_at: stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store.updated_at)
archived_at: optional stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store.archived_at)
description: optional stringî‚š
Free-text description of what the store contains, up to 1024 characters. Included in the agent's system prompt when the store is attached, so word it to be useful to the agent. Empty string when unset.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store.description)
metadata: optional map[string]î‚š
Arbitrary key-value tags for your own bookkeeping (such as the end user a store belongs to). Up to 16 pairs; keys 1â€“64 characters; values up to 512 characters. Returned on retrieve/list but not filterable.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store.metadata)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_store)

BetaManagedAgentsConflictError object { type, message } î‚š
type: "conflict_error"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_conflict_error.type)
message: optional stringî‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_conflict_error.message)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_conflict_error)
BetaManagedAgentsContentSha256Precondition object { type, content_sha256 } î‚š
Optimistic-concurrency precondition: the update applies only if the memory's stored `content_sha256` equals the supplied value. On mismatch, the request returns `memory_precondition_failed_error` (HTTP 409); re-read the memory and retry against the fresh state. If the precondition fails but the stored state already exactly matches the requested `content` and `path`, the server returns 200 instead of 409.
type: "content_sha256"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_content_sha256_precondition.type)
content_sha256: optional stringî‚š
Expected `content_sha256` of the stored memory (64 lowercase hexadecimal characters). Typically the `content_sha256` returned by a prior read or list call. Because the server applies no content normalization, clients can also compute this locally as the SHA-256 of the UTF-8 content bytes.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_content_sha256_precondition.content_sha256)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_content_sha256_precondition)
BetaManagedAgentsDeletedMemory object { id, type } î‚š
Tombstone returned by [Delete a memory](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/memory_stores/memories/delete). The memory's version history persists and remains listable via [List memory versions](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/memory_stores/memory_versions/list) until the store itself is deleted.
ID of the deleted memory (a `mem_...` value).
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_deleted_memory.id)
type: "memory_deleted"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_deleted_memory.type)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_deleted_memory)

BetaManagedAgentsMemory object { id, content_sha256, content_size_bytes, 7 more } î‚š
A `memory` object: a single text document at a hierarchical path inside a memory store. The `content` field is populated when `view=full` and `null` when `view=basic`; the `content_size_bytes` and `content_sha256` fields are always populated so sync clients can diff without fetching content. Memories are addressed by their `mem_...` ID; the path is the create key and can be changed via update.
Unique identifier for this memory (a `mem_...` value). Stable across renames; use this ID, not the path, to read, update, or delete the memory.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.id)
content_sha256: stringî‚š
Lowercase hex SHA-256 digest of the UTF-8 `content` bytes (64 characters). The server applies no normalization, so clients can compute the same hash locally for staleness checks and as the value for a `content_sha256` precondition on update. Always populated, regardless of `view`.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.content_sha256)
content_size_bytes: numberî‚š
Size of `content` in bytes (the UTF-8 plaintext length). Always populated, regardless of `view`.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.content_size_bytes)
created_at: stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.created_at)
memory_store_id: stringî‚š
ID of the memory store this memory belongs to (a `memstore_...` value).
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.memory_store_id)
memory_version_id: stringî‚š
ID of the `memory_version` representing this memory's current content (a `memver_...` value). This is the authoritative head pointer; `memory_version` objects do not carry an `is_latest` flag, so compare against this field instead. Enumerate the full history via [List memory versions](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/memory_stores/memory_versions/list).
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.memory_version_id)
path: stringî‚š
Hierarchical path of the memory within the store, e.g. `/projects/foo/notes.md`. Always starts with `/`. Paths are case-sensitive and unique within a store. Maximum 1,024 bytes.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.path)
type: "memory"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.type)
updated_at: stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.updated_at)
content: optional stringî‚š
The memory's UTF-8 text content. Populated when `view=full`; `null` when `view=basic`. Maximum 100 kB (102,400 bytes).
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.content)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory)
BetaManagedAgentsMemoryListItem = [BetaManagedAgentsMemory](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory) { id, content_sha256, content_size_bytes, 7 more }  or [BetaManagedAgentsMemoryPrefix](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_prefix) { path, type } î‚š
One item in a [List memories](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/memory_stores/memories/list) response: either a `memory` object or, when `depth` is set, a `memory_prefix` rollup marker.
BetaManagedAgentsMemory object { id, content_sha256, content_size_bytes, 7 more } î‚š
A `memory` object: a single text document at a hierarchical path inside a memory store. The `content` field is populated when `view=full` and `null` when `view=basic`; the `content_size_bytes` and `content_sha256` fields are always populated so sync clients can diff without fetching content. Memories are addressed by their `mem_...` ID; the path is the create key and can be changed via update.
Unique identifier for this memory (a `mem_...` value). Stable across renames; use this ID, not the path, to read, update, or delete the memory.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.id)
content_sha256: stringî‚š
Lowercase hex SHA-256 digest of the UTF-8 `content` bytes (64 characters). The server applies no normalization, so clients can compute the same hash locally for staleness checks and as the value for a `content_sha256` precondition on update. Always populated, regardless of `view`.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.content_sha256)
content_size_bytes: numberî‚š
Size of `content` in bytes (the UTF-8 plaintext length). Always populated, regardless of `view`.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.content_size_bytes)
created_at: stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.created_at)
memory_store_id: stringî‚š
ID of the memory store this memory belongs to (a `memstore_...` value).
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.memory_store_id)
memory_version_id: stringî‚š
ID of the `memory_version` representing this memory's current content (a `memver_...` value). This is the authoritative head pointer; `memory_version` objects do not carry an `is_latest` flag, so compare against this field instead. Enumerate the full history via [List memory versions](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/memory_stores/memory_versions/list).
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.memory_version_id)
path: stringî‚š
Hierarchical path of the memory within the store, e.g. `/projects/foo/notes.md`. Always starts with `/`. Paths are case-sensitive and unique within a store. Maximum 1,024 bytes.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.path)
type: "memory"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.type)
updated_at: stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.updated_at)
content: optional stringî‚š
The memory's UTF-8 text content. Populated when `view=full`; `null` when `view=basic`. Maximum 100 kB (102,400 bytes).
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory.content)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory)
BetaManagedAgentsMemoryPrefix object { path, type } î‚š
A rolled-up directory marker returned by [List memories](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/memory_stores/memories/list) when `depth` is set. Indicates that one or more memories exist deeper than the requested depth under this prefix. This is a list-time rollup, not a stored resource; it has no ID and no lifecycle. Each prefix counts toward the page `limit` and interleaves with `memory` items in path order.
path: stringî‚š
The rolled-up path prefix, including a trailing `/` (e.g. `/projects/foo/`). Pass this value as `path_prefix` on a subsequent list call to drill into the directory.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_prefix.path)
type: "memory_prefix"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_prefix.type)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_prefix)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_list_item)

[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_precondition_failed_error.type)

[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_precondition_failed_error.message)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_precondition_failed_error)
BetaManagedAgentsMemoryPrefix object { path, type } î‚š
A rolled-up directory marker returned by [List memories](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/memory_stores/memories/list) when `depth` is set. Indicates that one or more memories exist deeper than the requested depth under this prefix. This is a list-time rollup, not a stored resource; it has no ID and no lifecycle. Each prefix counts toward the page `limit` and interleaves with `memory` items in path order.
path: stringî‚š
The rolled-up path prefix, including a trailing `/` (e.g. `/projects/foo/`). Pass this value as `path_prefix` on a subsequent list call to drill into the directory.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_prefix.path)
type: "memory_prefix"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_prefix.type)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_prefix)
BetaManagedAgentsMemoryView = "basic" or "full"î‚š
Selects which projection of a `memory` or `memory_version` the server returns. `basic` returns the object with `content` set to `null`; `full` populates `content`. When omitted, the default is endpoint-specific: retrieve operations default to `full`; list, create, and update operations default to `basic`. Listing with `view=full` caps `limit` at 20.
"basic"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_view%5B0%5D)
"full"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_view%5B1%5D)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_view)
BetaManagedAgentsPrecondition object { type, content_sha256 } î‚š
Optimistic-concurrency precondition: the update applies only if the memory's stored `content_sha256` equals the supplied value. On mismatch, the request returns `memory_precondition_failed_error` (HTTP 409); re-read the memory and retry against the fresh state. If the precondition fails but the stored state already exactly matches the requested `content` and `path`, the server returns 200 instead of 409.
type: "content_sha256"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_precondition.type)
content_sha256: optional stringî‚š
Expected `content_sha256` of the stored memory (64 lowercase hexadecimal characters). Typically the `content_sha256` returned by a prior read or list call. Because the server applies no content normalization, clients can also compute this locally as the SHA-256 of the UTF-8 content bytes.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_precondition.content_sha256)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_precondition)
####  BetaMemory StoresMemory Versions
##### [List memory versions](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/memory_stores/memory_versions/list)
GET/v1/memory_stores/{memory_store_id}/memory_versions
##### [Retrieve a memory version](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/memory_stores/memory_versions/retrieve)
GET/v1/memory_stores/{memory_store_id}/memory_versions/{memory_version_id}
##### [Redact a memory version](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/memory_stores/memory_versions/redact)
POST/v1/memory_stores/{memory_store_id}/memory_versions/{memory_version_id}/redact
##### ModelsExpand Collapse 
BetaManagedAgentsActor = [BetaManagedAgentsSessionActor](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_session_actor) { session_id, type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsAPIActor](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_api_actor) { api_key_id, type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsUserActor](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_user_actor) { type, user_id } î‚š
Identifies who performed a write or redact operation. Captured at write time on the `memory_version` row. The API key that created a session is not recorded on agent writes; attribution answers who made the write, not who is ultimately responsible. Look up session provenance separately via the [Sessions API](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/sessions-retrieve).
BetaManagedAgentsSessionActor object { session_id, type } î‚š
Attribution for a write made by an agent during a session, through the mounted filesystem at `/mnt/memory/`.

ID of the session that performed the write (a `sesn_...` value). Look up the session via [Retrieve a session](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/sessions-retrieve) for further provenance.

BetaManagedAgentsMemoryVersion object { id, created_at, memory_id, 10 more } î‚š
A `memory_version` object: one immutable, attributed row in a memory's append-only history. Every non-no-op mutation to a memory produces a new version. Versions belong to the store (not the individual memory) and persist after the memory is deleted. Retrieving a redacted version returns 200 with `content`, `path`, `content_size_bytes`, and `content_sha256` set to `null`; branch on `redacted_at`, not HTTP status.
Unique identifier for this version (a `memver_...` value).
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_version.id)
created_at: stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_version.created_at)
memory_id: stringî‚š
ID of the memory this version snapshots (a `mem_...` value). Remains valid after the memory is deleted; pass it as `memory_id` to [List memory versions](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/memory_stores/memory_versions/list) to retrieve the full lineage including the `deleted` row.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_version.memory_id)
memory_store_id: stringî‚š
ID of the memory store this version belongs to (a `memstore_...` value).
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_version.memory_store_id)
operation: [BetaManagedAgentsMemoryVersionOperation](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_version_operation)î‚š
The kind of mutation a `memory_version` records. Every non-no-op mutation to a memory appends exactly one version row with one of these values.
"created"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_version.operation%20%2B%20\(resource\)%20beta.memory_stores.memory_versions%5B0%5D)

[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_version.operation%20%2B%20\(resource\)%20beta.memory_stores.memory_versions%5B1%5D)

[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_version.operation%20%2B%20\(resource\)%20beta.memory_stores.memory_versions%5B2%5D)

type: "memory_version"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_version.type)
content: optional stringî‚š
The memory's UTF-8 text content as of this version. `null` when `view=basic`, when `operation` is `deleted`, or when `redacted_at` is set.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_managed_agents_memory_version.content)

BetaUserProfile object { id, created_at, metadata, 6 more } î‚š
Unique identifier for this user profile, prefixed `uprof_`.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_user_profile.id)
created_at: stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_user_profile.created_at)
metadata: map[string]î‚š
Arbitrary key-value metadata. Maximum 16 pairs, keys up to 64 chars, values up to 512 chars.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_user_profile.metadata)
relationship: "external" or "resold" or "internal"î‚š
How the entity behind a user profile relates to the platform that owns the API key. `external`: an individual end-user of the platform. `resold`: a company the platform resells Claude access to. `internal`: the platform's own usage.
"external"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_user_profile.relationship%5B0%5D)
"resold"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_user_profile.relationship%5B1%5D)
"internal"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_user_profile.relationship%5B2%5D)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_user_profile.relationship)
trust_grants: map[[BetaUserProfileTrustGrant](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_user_profile_trust_grant) { status } ]î‚š
Trust grants for this profile, keyed by grant name. Key omitted when no grant is active or in flight.

Status of the trust grant.
"active"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_user_profile_trust_grant.status%5B0%5D)

[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_user_profile_trust_grant.status%5B1%5D)

[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_user_profile_trust_grant.status%5B2%5D)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_user_profile_trust_grant.status)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_user_profile.trust_grants)
type: "user_profile"î‚š
Object type. Always `user_profile`.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_user_profile.type)
updated_at: stringî‚š
A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

Platform's own identifier for this user. Not enforced unique.

####  BetaWebhooks
Helpers for receiving and verifying webhook events. Use `unwrap` in your SDK to verify signatures and parse payloads; see the [webhooks guide](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/managed-agents/webhooks) for handler examples.
Possible `data.type` values:
  * `session.archived`
  * `session.created`
  * `session.deleted`
  * `session.idled`
  * `session.outcome_evaluation_ended`
  * `session.pending`
  * `session.requires_action`
  * `session.running`
  * `session.status_idled`
  * `session.status_rescheduled`
  * `session.status_run_started`
  * `session.status_terminated`
  * `session.thread_created`
  * `session.thread_idled`
  * `session.thread_terminated`
  * `session.updated`
  * `vault.archived`
  * `vault.created`
  * `vault.deleted`
  * `vault_credential.archived`
  * `vault_credential.created`
  * `vault_credential.deleted`
  * `vault_credential.refresh_failed`

##### ModelsExpand Collapse

type: "event"î‚š

[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_webhook_event)
BetaWebhookEventData = [BetaWebhookSessionCreatedEventData](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_webhook_session_created_event_data) { id, organization_id, type, workspace_id }  or [BetaWebhookSessionPendingEventData](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_webhook_session_pending_event_data) { id, organization_id, type, workspace_id }  or [BetaWebhookSessionRunningEventData](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta#beta_webhook_session_running_event_data) { id, organization_id, type, workspace_id }  or 20 moreî‚š
